name: Indicative
skip_reason: Indicative has been acquired and rebranded; the official site now displays 'Indicative (Now mParticle Analytics)' and routes pricing/signup to mParticle's site, so it no longer operates as a standalone product under the Indicative name.

enrichment.short_description: LogSnag turns simple HTTP events into real-time dashboards, funnels, and instant push notifications for tracking product activity.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: LogSnag: real-time event tracking with dashboards, funnels, and push notifications for SaaS and product teams.

features.title: Event Tracking
features.description: Send events from any product via simple HTTP requests, with SDKs for multiple languages.

features.title: Real-time Dashboards
features.description: Converts tracked events into live dashboards and metrics.

features.title: Funnel Analysis
features.description: Tracks conversion rates and user journeys across event sequences.

features.title: Journey Tracking
features.description: Maps relationships between events for a given user, order, shipment, or other entity.

features.title: Push Notifications
features.description: Sends instant alerts for important events to desktop, mobile, and browser.

features.title: Advanced Filtering
features.description: Locate specific events and KPIs from tracked activity.

pros: Free forever plan with no credit card required
Simple HTTP-based event integration
Real-time push notifications for tracked events
Funnel and journey analysis included on paid plans
Unlimited seats on all paid tiers
cons: Free plan capped at 2,500 events/month and 3 seats
Enterprise plan requires a custom quote rather than published pricing
No founding year or headquarters information published on the site

faqs.question: Does LogSnag have a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, LogSnag offers a free forever plan with 3 seats and 2,500 events per month, no credit card required.

faqs.question: How do I send events to LogSnag?
faqs.answer: Events are sent via simple HTTP requests, with SDKs available for common languages.

faqs.question: What is Journey Tracking?
faqs.answer: Journey Tracking maps relationships between events tied to the same user, order, or other entity to visualize activity over time.

faqs.question: Is there an annual billing discount?
faqs.answer: Yes, all paid plans include the equivalent of 2 months free when billed annually.

use_cases.title: SaaS event monitoring
use_cases.description: Track signups, billing events, and errors in real time across a SaaS product.

use_cases.title: Order and shipment tracking
use_cases.description: Follow an order or shipment's journey through multiple events.

use_cases.title: Solo founder alerts
use_cases.description: Get instant push notifications for key events like new signups or payments.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is LogSnag?
paragraphs: LogSnag is an event tracking and monitoring tool that lets teams send events from any product via HTTP requests and turn them into real-time dashboards, funnels, and journeys.
Beyond raw event logs, LogSnag adds push notifications so teams can be alerted the moment a meaningful event occurs, such as a new signup or a failed payment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who LogSnag is for
paragraphs: LogSnag is built for SaaS teams, solo founders, and product teams that want lightweight event tracking and real-time alerts without setting up a full analytics stack.

enrichment.short_description: Pirsch is a privacy-focused, cookieless web analytics tool that tracks site traffic without consent banners.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Pirsch offers GDPR-compliant, cookieless website analytics with funnels, events, and a 30-day free trial.

features.title: Cookieless tracking
features.description: Tracks visitors without cookies, so no consent banner is required.

features.title: Events & conversion goals
features.description: Tracks custom events and conversion goals alongside standard traffic metrics.

features.title: Funnel analysis
features.description: Visualizes multi-step conversion funnels and individual session exploration.

features.title: Tag-based segmentation
features.description: Segments visitors using custom tags.

features.title: Webhooks & API
features.description: RESTful API, SDKs, and webhook integrations for automation.

features.title: White-labeling
features.description: Custom domains and extensive white-label theming for agencies.

features.title: Data import
features.description: Imports historical data from Plausible, Fathom, and Google Analytics.

pros: No consent banners required (cookieless, GDPR/CCPA/PECR compliant)
RESTful API and SDKs included even on the entry-level plan
Can import historical data from Plausible, Fathom, and Google Analytics
Hosted on a dedicated server cluster in Germany with 100% data ownership
cons: No permanent free plan, only a 30-day free trial
Standard plan limited to 10,000 monthly page views and 50 websites
Funnels, A/B testing, and white-labeling are reserved for the Plus plan and above
Enterprise features like SSO and on-premise hosting require a custom quote

faqs.question: Does Pirsch require a cookie consent banner?
faqs.answer: No, Pirsch uses cookieless tracking so websites using it don't need a consent banner.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pirsch offers a 30-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Can I import data from Google Analytics?
faqs.answer: Yes, Pirsch supports data import from Plausible, Fathom, and Google Analytics.

faqs.question: Where is Pirsch hosted?
faqs.answer: Pirsch is made and hosted in Germany, using a secure server cluster in the country.

use_cases.title: GDPR-compliant site analytics
use_cases.description: Track website traffic without cookie banners while staying compliant with EU privacy law.

use_cases.title: Multi-client dashboards
use_cases.description: White-label and manage analytics across many client websites from one account.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Pirsch?
paragraphs: Pirsch is a privacy-focused web analytics platform positioned as an alternative to Google Analytics. It tracks visitor behavior without cookies, so sites using it don't need to show a consent banner.
The platform covers events, conversion goals, funnels, and session-level analysis, and is hosted on servers in Germany with GDPR, CCPA, and PECR compliance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Pirsch is for
paragraphs: Pirsch suits website owners and developers who want privacy-compliant analytics, as well as agencies managing multiple client sites that need white-label reporting.

enrichment.short_description: Fathom Analytics offers simple, privacy-first website analytics with no cookie banners and permanent data retention.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Fathom Analytics is a privacy-first, cookieless web analytics tool used by over a million websites.

features.title: Single-line install
features.description: Adds to any CMS or framework with one line of code.

features.title: Event tracking
features.description: Tracks user interactions like sign-ups, clicks, and sales.

features.title: Email reporting
features.description: Sends scheduled email reports of site performance.

features.title: UTM campaign tracking
features.description: Tracks campaign performance via UTM parameters.

features.title: Forever data retention
features.description: Keeps analytics data permanently rather than expiring it.

features.title: Lightweight script
features.description: A 2kB tracking script designed not to hurt page speed or SEO.

features.title: Analytics API
features.description: Provides API access for developers, rate-limited to 600 requests/hour.

pros: Cookieless and anonymous IP tracking, no cookie banner needed
GDPR, CCPA, and ePrivacy compliant
Lightweight 2kB script that doesn't slow down pages
Independently owned with no venture capital backing
Used by over one million websites including IBM and GitHub
cons: No permanent free plan, only a 7-day free trial
Entry pricing starts around $15/month and scales with pageview volume
Higher pageview tiers (1M+) require contacting sales rather than published pricing
Extra sites beyond the included 50 cost an additional $10/month per pack of 50

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Fathom Analytics offers a 7-day unlimited free trial with no obligation.

faqs.question: Does Fathom use cookies?
faqs.answer: No, Fathom is cookieless and tracks anonymized IP data, so no cookie banner is required.

faqs.question: How long is data retained?
faqs.answer: Fathom retains analytics data forever rather than expiring it after a set period.

faqs.question: How many sites are included?
faqs.answer: Plans include 50 sites, with additional sites available in packs of 50 for $10/month.

use_cases.title: Privacy-compliant site analytics
use_cases.description: Measure traffic and events without cookie banners or consent friction.

use_cases.title: Agency and multi-site reporting
use_cases.description: Manage analytics for many websites under one account.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Fathom Analytics?
paragraphs: Fathom Analytics is a privacy-first web analytics tool that provides simple traffic dashboards without the complexity or privacy concerns of tools like Google Analytics.
It installs with a single line of code, tracks events and UTM campaigns, and retains data permanently, all without using cookies.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Fathom Analytics is for
paragraphs: Fathom is aimed at website owners and developers, from startups to large companies, who want straightforward, privacy-compliant analytics without complex reporting structures.

enrichment.short_description: Simple Analytics tracks website traffic without cookies or personal data, hosted entirely in the EU.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Simple Analytics is a privacy-first, cookie-free web analytics tool with EU data hosting and a free tier.

features.title: Cookieless tracking
features.description: Collects traffic data without cookies or personal data, no consent banner needed.

features.title: Full traffic capture
features.description: Aims to measure a higher share of visitors than tools blocked by consent requirements or ad-blockers.

features.title: EU-hosted data
features.description: Keeps all analytics data hosted in Europe.

features.title: GA4 compatibility
features.description: Can run alongside an existing Google Analytics installation.

features.title: AI assistant
features.description: Includes an AI-powered assistant for exploring analytics data.

features.title: One-page dashboard
features.description: Presents traffic data on a single page without complex configuration.

pros: Forever-free tier available
No cookies, consent banners, or personal data collected
EU-hosted infrastructure with GDPR, PECR, and CCPA compliance
14-day free trial on paid plans with no credit card required
cons: Free plan limited to 1 user, 5 websites, and 1 month of history
Paid plan charges €20/month per additional user
Enterprise plan requires a custom demo/quote rather than published pricing
SOC 2 compliance is listed as 'coming soon' rather than in place

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Simple Analytics offers a forever-free tier limited to 1 user across 5 websites with 1 month of history.

faqs.question: Does Simple Analytics use cookies?
faqs.answer: No, it collects traffic data without cookies or personal data, so no consent banner is required.

faqs.question: Where is data hosted?
faqs.answer: All data is hosted in the EU.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for paid plans?
faqs.answer: Yes, a 14-day free trial is available with no credit card required.

use_cases.title: Privacy-minded site analytics
use_cases.description: Track traffic without cookies or consent banners while staying GDPR compliant.

use_cases.title: Recovering lost traffic data
use_cases.description: Capture visitor data missed by consent-gated or ad-blocked tools, used alongside existing GA4 setups.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Simple Analytics?
paragraphs: Simple Analytics is a privacy-focused web analytics platform that collects traffic data without cookies or personal data, so sites using it don't need consent banners.
The company positions its full traffic capture as recovering visitors missed by consent-gated or ad-blocked analytics tools, while keeping all data hosted in the EU.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Simple Analytics is for
paragraphs: It's built for privacy-minded teams and agencies, as well as larger enterprises like Bloomberg and GOV.UK that need EU-hosted, consent-free analytics alongside existing tools.

enrichment.short_description: Cabin is a privacy-first, cookie-free web analytics tool built with a lightweight script and carbon reporting.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Cabin offers cookie-free website analytics with carbon emissions reporting and EU-hosted data.

features.title: Lightweight script
features.description: Tracking script described as 77 times smaller than Google Analytics.

features.title: Cookie-free tracking
features.description: No cookies, IP tracking, or browser fingerprinting used.

features.title: CO2 reporting
features.description: Tracks estimated carbon emissions related to website traffic.

features.title: Custom events & UTM tracking
features.description: Tracks custom events and UTM campaign parameters.

features.title: Weekly email reports
features.description: Sends automated weekly traffic summaries by email.

features.title: Public dashboards
features.description: Supports public, shareable dashboards on custom domains.

pros: Free tier with unlimited page views under fair use
No cookies, IP tracking, or browser fingerprinting
Unique carbon emissions reporting for sustainability-focused teams
GDPR, CCPA, ePrivacy, and PECR compliant
Data encrypted and stored exclusively in the EU
cons: Free tier limited to 1 website and 30 days of data retention
Unlimited websites and retention require the $19/month Pro plan
No founding year, headquarters, or company size published on the site

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Cabin's Basic tier is free and includes 1 website with 30 days of data retention.

faqs.question: Does Cabin use cookies?
faqs.answer: No, Cabin tracks visitors without cookies, IP tracking, or browser fingerprinting.

faqs.question: What is CO2 reporting?
faqs.answer: Cabin includes carbon emissions reporting tied to website traffic as part of its sustainability focus.

faqs.question: Where is data stored?
faqs.answer: Data is end-to-end encrypted and stored exclusively in the EU.

use_cases.title: Sustainability-conscious analytics
use_cases.description: Track website traffic while also monitoring estimated carbon emissions.

use_cases.title: Basic privacy-first analytics
use_cases.description: Get cookie-free traffic analytics for a single site at no cost.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Cabin?
paragraphs: Cabin is a privacy-focused web analytics tool that tracks website performance without cookies, IP tracking, or browser fingerprinting.
It differentiates itself with a very lightweight tracking script and built-in carbon emissions reporting, alongside standard features like custom events and weekly email reports.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Cabin is for
paragraphs: Cabin serves organizations ranging from solo developers to large organizations that want ethical, privacy-first analytics with a sustainability angle.

enrichment.short_description: Statsig unifies feature flags, A/B testing, product analytics, and session replay in one platform.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Statsig combines experimentation, feature flags, product analytics, and session replay for product teams.

features.title: Experimentation
features.description: Runs A/B tests with statistical analysis at scale.

features.title: Feature flags
features.description: Controls feature releases and rollouts linked to product data.

features.title: Product analytics
features.description: Builds metrics and tracks user behavior.

features.title: Session replay
features.description: Records visual sessions linked to experiments and feature flags.

features.title: Warehouse native
features.description: Integrates directly with existing data warehouses.

features.title: Broad SDK support
features.description: Provides SDKs for 18+ programming languages and frameworks.

pros: Generous free Developer tier with 2M events/month and unlimited flag checks
Unlimited seats even on the free plan
Warehouse-native architecture for direct data warehouse integration
Used by companies including OpenAI, Brex, and Notion
SOC 2 Type 2 certified
cons: Pro plan bills a $150/month baseline plus overages beyond 5M events
Enterprise pricing is custom/quote-only
Advanced experimentation and unlimited data retention require the Pro plan or above

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Developer tier is free and includes 2M events/month, unlimited flag checks, and unlimited seats.

faqs.question: What does the Pro plan cost?
faqs.answer: Pro is $150/month, including 5M events, with additional events billed at $0.05 per 1,000.

faqs.question: Does Statsig support session replay?
faqs.answer: Yes, session replay is included and can be linked to experiments and feature flags.

faqs.question: How many SDKs does Statsig support?
faqs.answer: Statsig provides SDKs for 18+ programming languages and frameworks.

use_cases.title: Running product experiments
use_cases.description: Launch and analyze A/B tests with statistical rigor.

use_cases.title: Controlled feature rollouts
use_cases.description: Ship features gradually using feature flags tied to product data.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Statsig?
paragraphs: Statsig is a unified product development platform that combines experimentation, feature flags, product analytics, and session replay in a single system.
It supports warehouse-native deployments so teams can run experiments and analyze behavior directly against their existing data warehouse.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Statsig is for
paragraphs: Statsig serves engineering teams, data scientists, and product managers across industries like gaming, B2B SaaS, and e-commerce who need to test and roll out features safely.

enrichment.short_description: GrowthBook is a warehouse-native platform for running A/B tests, managing feature flags, and product analytics.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: GrowthBook lets teams run experiments and manage feature flags directly on their own data warehouse.

features.title: Experimentation
features.description: Runs A/B tests at scale with statistical modeling.

features.title: Feature flags
features.description: Ships features safely with auto-rollbacks and gradual ramp schedules.

features.title: Product analytics with AI Data Analyst
features.description: Explores user behavior and lets users ask an AI assistant questions about the data.

features.title: Warehouse-native architecture
features.description: Queries metrics directly from an existing data warehouse rather than a separate store.

features.title: Broad SDK support
features.description: Supports 24+ SDKs including JavaScript, Python, React, Vue, iOS, and Android.

features.title: AI Visual Editor
features.description: Builds experiments visually with AI assistance.

features.title: MCP Server & REST API
features.description: Integrates with AI agents and external tools via an MCP server and REST API.

pros: Free Starter plan for up to 3 users, plus a free open-source self-hosted option
Warehouse-native: bring your own data warehouse instead of duplicating data
Broad SDK support across 24+ languages and platforms
Used by 3,000+ companies
Self-hosted open-source version available with unlimited users
cons: Pro plan is billed per seat ($40/seat/month), which scales with team size
CDN request and bandwidth overages are billed beyond included limits
Enterprise features (SSO, SCIM, advanced access control) require a custom quote

faqs.question: Is GrowthBook free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Starter cloud plan is free for up to 3 users, and the open-source self-hosted version is also free with unlimited users.

faqs.question: What does the Pro plan cost?
faqs.answer: Pro is $40 per seat per month for up to 50 users.

faqs.question: Does GrowthBook require its own data warehouse?
faqs.answer: No, GrowthBook can connect to an existing data warehouse or use a managed warehouse option.

faqs.question: Can I self-host GrowthBook?
faqs.answer: Yes, GrowthBook offers a free open-source self-hosted version as well as a self-hosted Enterprise tier.

use_cases.title: Running experiments on existing data
use_cases.description: Analyze A/B tests directly against a company's own data warehouse.

use_cases.title: Controlled feature releases
use_cases.description: Roll out features gradually with auto-rollback protection.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is GrowthBook?
paragraphs: GrowthBook is a warehouse-native platform for running experiments, managing feature flags, and analyzing product data, connecting directly to a team's existing data warehouse.
It offers both a hosted cloud version and a free open-source self-hosted version, with SDKs covering 24+ languages and platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who GrowthBook is for
paragraphs: GrowthBook serves engineers who need lightweight SDKs, data teams that want SQL-based metric definitions, and product managers who want to run experiments and control rollouts without engineering dependencies.

enrichment.short_description: JumpCloud is a cloud directory platform unifying identity, device, and access management for IT teams.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: JumpCloud unifies cloud directory, SSO, MFA, and device management for hybrid IT environments.

features.title: Cloud directory
features.description: Centralizes user lifecycle management and HRIS integration.

features.title: Single sign-on & MFA
features.description: Provides SSO, multi-factor authentication, and conditional access.

features.title: Privileged access & password manager
features.description: Manages privileged access and includes a password manager.

features.title: Cross-platform device management
features.description: Manages endpoints across Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android.

features.title: Shadow AI & SaaS management
features.description: Discovers SaaS usage, governs shadow AI, and optimizes SaaS spend.

features.title: IT automation
features.description: Automates IT workflows via APIs for orchestration.

pros: 30-day fully-featured free trial with no credit card required
A la carte pricing lets teams pay only for the modules they need
Cross-platform device management spanning Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android
Special pricing available for education, nonprofit, and MSP customers
cons: No permanent free plan is offered
Platform-tier packages require contacting sales rather than published pricing
No founding year or headquarters information published on the pages reviewed

faqs.question: Does JumpCloud offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, JumpCloud offers a 30-day fully-featured trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: What does JumpCloud cost?
faqs.answer: Package pricing starts at $9/user/month (annual) for Device Management, with SSO and combined packages priced higher; Platform tiers require contacting sales.

faqs.question: Which platforms does JumpCloud manage?
faqs.answer: JumpCloud manages devices across Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android.

faqs.question: Does JumpCloud offer discounts?
faqs.answer: Yes, JumpCloud offers special pricing for education, nonprofits, and MSP partners.

use_cases.title: Unified IT management for hybrid teams
use_cases.description: Manage identity, devices, and access from a single directory across a distributed workforce.

use_cases.title: Multi-client IT administration
use_cases.description: Administer identity and device management across multiple client organizations.

features: Device management plus identity features and MFA
Identity, device, SSO, and passwordless authentication
Unified identity, device, and access management

long_form_blocks.heading: What is JumpCloud?
paragraphs: JumpCloud is a unified platform for managing devices and access for human and non-human identities, combining a cloud directory, SSO, MFA, and cross-platform device management in one system.
It offers modular, a la carte pricing so organizations can purchase only the identity, access, or device management components they need.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who JumpCloud is for
paragraphs: JumpCloud is aimed at organizations with hybrid or distributed workforces, including enterprises and managed service providers (MSPs) modernizing their IT operations.

enrichment.short_description: Duo, by Cisco, provides multi-factor authentication, SSO, and identity security to protect workforce access.
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Duo Security offers MFA, SSO, and identity threat protection, with a free tier for up to 10 users.

features.title: Phishing-resistant MFA
features.description: Provides multi-factor authentication and passwordless authentication without extra hardware.

features.title: Single sign-on
features.description: Unifies access across applications with SSO.

features.title: Duo Directory
features.description: Centralizes identity management.

features.title: Device Trust
features.description: Verifies device health and trust before granting access.

features.title: Cisco Identity Intelligence
features.description: Adds threat detection across identity and access activity.

features.title: Identity Threat Detection & Response (ITDR)
features.description: Detects and responds to identity-based threats.

features.title: Agentic IAM
features.description: Extends identity and access management controls to AI agents.

pros: Free tier available for teams of 10 or fewer users
Tiered pricing from $3 to $9/user/month as needs grow
Phishing-resistant MFA and passwordless authentication included from the Essentials tier
Backed by Cisco's security infrastructure
Recognized as a Gartner Customers' Choice for Access Management
cons: Advanced features like Cisco Identity Intelligence and Zero Trust access require the higher Advantage or Premier tiers
VPN-less remote access and complete device trust are reserved for the top Premier plan
No founding year or headquarters information published on the pages reviewed

faqs.question: Is there a free plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Duo Free is available at no cost for teams of 10 or fewer users.

faqs.question: What are Duo's paid plans?
faqs.answer: Duo offers three paid editions: Essentials ($3/user/month), Advantage ($6/user/month), and Premier ($9/user/month).

faqs.question: Does Duo require extra hardware?
faqs.answer: No, Duo's MFA and passwordless authentication work without additional hardware tokens.

faqs.question: Who owns Duo Security?
faqs.answer: Duo Security is owned by Cisco Systems, Inc.

use_cases.title: Basic MFA for small teams
use_cases.description: Add strong multi-factor authentication for up to 10 users at no cost.

use_cases.title: Enterprise identity security
use_cases.description: Combine MFA, SSO, device trust, and threat detection for larger organizations.

long_form_blocks.heading: What is Duo Security?
paragraphs: Duo Security, part of Cisco, is an identity and access management platform centered on phishing-resistant MFA, single sign-on, and device trust.
It scales from a free tier for small teams up to enterprise editions that add identity threat detection, Zero Trust access, and VPN-less remote access.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Duo Security is for
paragraphs: Duo serves organizations of many sizes, from small and medium businesses to enterprises, government agencies, and managed service providers across healthcare, finance, and education.
